description: Rural Development funds will be used to help the Little Crow Telemedia Network located in Hutchinson, MN and River Valley Education District located in Chippewa, MN, provide technology and distance learning opportunities for their member districts. Distance learning is a critical need and offers significant opportunities for 19 participating districts and other partners, including the Southwest Adult Basic Education-Granite Falls Region and the Upper Sioux Community. The distance learning network will offer increased opportunities for a more comprehensive set of course offerings for high school students and offer college prep and college courses in the schools, and AP courses via distance learning so that more students have access to them. This project is a distance learning initiative serving 19 school districts, two Adult Basic Education sites, and the Upper Sioux Community. An estimated 10,000 rural students, 600 rural educators, 50 Adult Basic Education (ABE) students and more than 400 Upper Sioux Community tribal members will have access to programming through this project.

description: Rural Development funds will be used to help create a distance learning network in Forrest, Amite and Marion Counties in Mississippi. The Hub will be Southern Regional Educational Service Agency, Hattiesburg, MS in Forrest County. End-user sites will be at Amite County High School, Amite County Elementary School, Liberty and Amite County Vo-Tech, Liberty, all in Amite County, East Marion High School, rural Marion County and West Marion High School in Foxworth, also in Marion County. The network will provide enhanced educational opportunities not previously available in the area. Schools will be able to interconnect, and share teaching staff. description: Rural Development funds will help fund the Lea County Distance Education distance learning project located on the campus of New Mexico Junior College in Hobbs, NM and will connect to four end-user sites located in Tatum, Eunice, Lovington and Jal, New Mexico. Rural Development funds will be used to help Northeastern Technical College with campuses in Cheraw, SC and Dillon, SC to establish a distance learning project that will provide virtual connections to classrooms located in Dillon, Lake View and Latta, SC. The hubs and end-user sites are located in Chesterfield and Dillon Counties. The project will allow the project schools to connect to other counties, located near and/or far, and connect to other schools, colleges, and organizations in order to bring needed educational content to teachers and students. The project will allow for expanded educational opportunities to provide students with core courses, electives, dual credit courses, and advanced placement and will also allow for community meetings, continuing education, and training for first responders.

Rural Development funds will be used to help create a distance learning network in Marlboro and Chesterfield Counties, South Carolina. Northeastern Technical College campuses at Bennettsville and Pageland, SC will be the hub/end-user sites, and high schools at Pageland, Chesterfield, McBee and Cheraw will be end-user sites. Expanded course offerings will include core course, dual credit courses and advanced placement courses. The Learning Network will also provide a community resource for workforce development, job training, community meetings and continuing education for first responders. Advanced manufacturing mechatronics type courses will also be offered, which will enhance local manufacturing job opportunities.

These opportunities will include dual enrollment classes, virtual field trips, classroom collaborations, career explorations, and an expanded menu of course offerings at all levels. Teachers will be able to participate in professional learning communities and access development opportunities without travel.

description: Rural Development funds will help the University of Washington in King County, WA purchase telemedicine equipment to provide enhanced telemedicine services for its The RIDE (Regional Initiatives in Dental Education) tele-dentistry program. The project will benefit one hub and 11 end-user sites in 12 communities, throughout Washington and Montana, by providing tele dentistry mobile carts, intraoral cameras, video conferencing equipment and software. The RIDE program trains students to be placed in rural dental practices after they graduate. It was developed to address oral health workforce needs in rural and underserved communities. RIDE students are sent on rural service learning rotations for five to six months during their four years of dental school. While all dental students go on five-week rotations, only RIDE students are required to do so in rural locations for a prolonged period of time. The equipment purchased will allow the students to continue their education while serving in the rural communities.
